   graph_outputs = OrderedSet(V.graph.get_output_names())\n\n        default_peak_memory, name_to_freeable_input_buf = prepare_planning_info(\n            nodes,\n            self.name_to_buf,\n            self.name_to_fused_node,\n            OrderedSet(V.graph.graph_inputs.keys()),\n            graph_outputs,\n        )\n\n        reordered_nodes = self.reorder_for_minimizing_partition(nodes)\n        reorder_peak_memory, _ = estimate_peak_memory(\n            reordered_nodes, name_to_freeable_input_buf, graph_outputs\n        )\n\n        # 1.1 here means 10% extra peak memory budget which is quite arbitrary\n        if reorder_peak_memory < default_peak_memory * 1.1:\n            return reordered_nodes\n\n        return nodes\n\n    def reorder_for_partition_with_simple_dependency(\n        self, nodes: list[BaseSchedulerNode]\n    ) -> list[BaseSchedulerNode]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Reorder a node if it should be partitioned and has simple dependency:\n        1. move a partitioned node to the front if it has no dependency\n        2. move a partitioned node to the back if it is only used by OutputNode\n        3. otherwise do not reorder\n        \"\"\"\n\n        front: list[BaseSchedulerNode] = []\n        middle: list[BaseSchedulerNode] = []\n        back: list[BaseSchedulerNode] = []\n\n        def only_output_user(node: BaseSchedulerNode) -> bool:\n            for buf in node.get_outputs():\n                for use in buf.users:\n                    if not isinstance(use.node, OutputNode):\n                        return False\n            return True\n\n        for node in nodes:\n            should_partition = self.should_partition(node)\n            if should_partition and len(node.unmet_dependencies) == 0:\n                front.append(node)\n            elif should_partition and only_output_user(node):\n                back.append(node)\n            else:\n                middle.append(node)\n\n        return front + middle + back\n\n    def graph_partition(\n        self,\n    ) -> tuple[list[PartitionType], list[GraphPartitionSignature]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Given a list of BaseSchedulerNodes, split into a list of\n        graph partitions and compute partition input/output signatures.\n        \"\"\"\n        partitions: list[PartitionType] = []\n        skip_cudagraph = True\n        cur_partition: PartitionType = []\n        skip_cudagraphs = []\n        for node in self.nodes:\n            should_partition = self.should_partition(node, should_log=True)\n            if cur_partition and skip_cudagraph != should_partition:\n                partitions.append(cur_partition)\n                skip_cudagraphs.append(skip_cudagraph)\n                cur_partition = []\n\n            skip_cudagraph = should_partition\n            cur_partition.append(node)\n\n        if cur_partition:\n            partitions.append(cur_partition)\n            skip_cudagraphs.append(skip_cudagraph)\n\n        signatures = self.get_graph_partition_signature(\n            partitions=partitions, skip_cudagraphs=skip_cudagraphs\n        )\n        self.compute_graph_partition_maps(signatures)\n\n        return partitions, signatures\n\n    def codegen(self) -> None:\n        with dynamo_timed(\"Scheduler.codegen\"):\n            return (\n                self._codegen_partitions()\n                if torch._inductor.config.graph_partition\n                else self._codegen(self.nodes)\n            )\n\n    def _codegen_partition_wrapper(\n        self,\n        partition: PartitionType,\n        signature: GraphPartitionSignature,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Codegen a partition given its inputs/outputs\"\"\"\n        from .codegen.wrapper import SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen\n\n        parent_wrapper_code = V.graph.wrapper_code\n        graph_partition_id = next(self._graph_partition_counter)\n\n        with V.graph.set_current_wrapper_code():\n            V.graph.init_wrapper_code(\n                is_subgraph=True,\n                subgraph_name=f\"partition_{graph_partition_id}\",\n                parent_wrapper_code=parent_wrapper_code,\n                partition_signatures=signature,\n            )\n            self._codegen(partition)\n\n            # Note: [Removed Graph Partition Arguments]\n            # Graph partition relies on node.read_writes to analyze the partition\n            # inputs and outputs. However, during codegen, we may decide some buffers\n            # are internal to a kernel (e.g., triton kernel) such that these buffers\n            # are never actually defined. This information is collected during codegen\n            # and recorded in V.graph.removed_buffers. So we cleanup signature and write\n            # prefix (i.e., generating call function and return outputs) after we have\n            # codegen the partition.\n            assert isinstance(V.graph.wrapper_code, SubgraphPythonWrapperCodegen)\n            signature = self.clean_removed_buffer_from_partition_signatures(signature)\n            V.graph.wrapper_code.partition_signatures = signature\n            V.graph.wrapper_code.write_prefix()\n\n            partition_code, _ = V.graph.wrapper_code.generate(V.graph.is_inference)\n\n        V.graph.wrapper_code.define_subgraph_launcher_fn(partition_code.value)\n\n        V.graph.wrapper_code.codegen_partition_call(graph_partition_id, signature)\n        V.graph.wrapper_code.allocated.update(  # type: ignore[has-type]\n            [node.get_name() for node in signature.output_nodes]\n        )\n\n    def use_default_device_context(\n        self, partitions: list[PartitionType], signatures: list[GraphPartitionSignature]\n    ) -> contextlib.AbstractContextManager[None]:\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def ctx() -> Iterator[None]:\n            self.update_graph_partition_default_device(partitions, signatures)\n            if self.default_device_context and device_need_guard(\n                self.default_device_context.type\n            ):\n                assert self.default_device_context.index is not None, (\n                    \"device should have an index\"\n                )\n                V.graph.wrapper_code.codegen_device_guard_enter(\n                    self.default_device_context.index\n                )\n\n            try:\n                yield\n            finally:\n                if self.default_device_context and device_need_guard(\n                    self.default_device_context.type\n                ):\n                    V.graph.wrapper_code.codegen_device_guard_exit()\n                self.default_device_context = None\n\n        return ctx()\n\n    def update_graph_partition_default_device(\n        self, partitions: list[PartitionType], signatures: list[GraphPartitionSignature]\n    ) -> None:\n        # Note: [Graph Partition Device Contexts]\n        # Entering a device context takes 60 microseconds and exiting a device\n        # context takes 20 microseconds. If all graph partitions and\n        # cudagraph-unsafe ops happen on the same device, we can share the\n        # device context.\n\n        if len(partitions) == 1 and not signatures[0].skip_cudagraph:\n            # If there is only 1 cudagraph partition, the device context\n            # should happen within the cudagraph partition, which\n            # would be removed by cudagraph.\n            return\n\n        def get_cudagraph_partition_device(partition: PartitionType) -> torch.device:\n            partition_device = partition[0].get_device()\n            assert partition_device is not None\n            return partition_device\n\n        def all_on_target_device(\n            partition: PartitionType, target_device: torch.device\n        ) -> bool:\n            for node in partition:\n                device = node.get_device()\n                if device != target_device:\n                    return False\n            return True\n\n        cudagraph_partition_device = None\n        for partition, signature in zip(partitions, signatures):\n            if not signature.skip_cudagraph:\n                cudagraph_partition_device = get_cudagraph_partition_device(partition)\n                break\n\n        # all partitions skip cudagraph\n        if cudagraph_partition_device is None:\n            return\n\n        for partition, signature in zip(partitions, signatures):\n            if signature.skip_cudagraph and not all_on_target_device(\n                partition, cudagraph_partition_device\n            ):\n                return\n\n        self.default_device_context = cudagraph_partition_device\n\n    def _codegen_partitions(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Split nodes into partitions and codegen each partition into separate functions.\n        This allows further applying different optimizations (e.g., cudagraph) to\n        each function.\n        \"\"\"\n        partitions, signatures = self.graph_partition()\n\n        if len(partitions) > 1:\n            msg = f\"cudagraph partition into {len(partitions)} partitions\"\n            maybe_log_cudagraph_partition(msg=msg, prefix=\"\")\n\n        with self.use_default_device_context(partitions, signatures):\n            for partition, signature in zip(partitions, signatures):\n                assert len(partition) >= 1, (\n                    f\"Each partition must have at least one node but found {len(partition)}\"\n                )\n\n                if signature.skip_cudagraph:\n                    self._codegen(partition)\n                else:\n                    self._codegen_partition_wrapper(partition, signature)\n\n        num_partitions = next(self._graph_partition_counter)\n        V.graph.wrapper_code.set_all_partition_names(num_partitions)\n\n        # See [Note: Graph Partition Map for CUDAGraph]\n        if num_partitions > 0:\n            assert V.graph.partition_maps is not None\n            assert num_partitions == len(V.graph.partition_maps), (\n                f\"Expect {num_partitions} partition maps but got {len(V.graph.partition_maps)}\"\n            )\n\n    def _codegen(self, nodes: list[BaseSchedulerNode]) -> None:\n        if config.check_stack_no_cycles_TESTING_ONLY:\n            import torch._dynamo.convert_frame\n\n            stack = traceback.extract_stack()\n            seen: OrderedSet[tuple[str, int | None]] = OrderedSet()\n            for frame in reversed(stack):\n                # This is where maybe_cprofile is\n                if (\n                    frame.name == \"_compile_inner\"\n                    and frame.filename == torch._dynamo.convert_frame.__file__\n                ):\n                    break\n                key = (frame.filename, frame.lineno)\n                assert key not in seen, (\n                    f\"Duplicate stack frame {frame.filename}:{frame.lineno}; \"\n                    \"did you add a decorator to one of the functions in this stack \"\n                    \"trace?  If so, try using a context manager instead.\"\n                )\n                seen.add(key)\n\n        self.current_device = self.default_device_context\n\n        if self.default_device_context and config.triton.autotune_at_compile_time:\n            V.graph.wrapper_code.write_get_raw_stream_header()\n\n        for node in nodes:\n            if log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\n                try:\n                    log.debug(\n                        \"Generating code for node %s with estimated runtime %f\",\n                        node.get_name(),\n                        node.get_estimated_runtime(),\n                    )\n                except Exception:\n                    log.debug(\n                        \"Generating code for node %s with estimated runtime 0.0\",\n                        node.get_name(),\n                    )\n\n            self.enter_context(node)\n\n            if device := node.get_device():\n                if (\n                    device != self.current_device\n                    or node.is_extern()\n                    or node.is_template()\n                ):\n                    self.flush()\n                if device != self.current_device:\n                    if self.current_device and device_need_guard(\n                        self.current_device.type\n                    ):\n                        V.graph.wrapper_code.codegen_device_guard_exit()\n                    self.current_device = device\n                    if device_need_guard(device.type):\n                        assert device.index is not None, \"device should have an index\"\n                        V.graph.wrapper_code.codegen_device_guard_enter(device.index)\n\n            self.current_node = node\n            self.buffer_names_to_free.update(node.last_usage)\n\n            if node.is_template():\n                prologue, template_node, epilogue = node.get_prologue_template_epilogue(\n                    list(node.get_nodes())\n                )\n                self.get_backend(device).codegen_template(\n                    template_node, epilogue, prologue\n                )\n            elif node.is_extern():\n                node = typing.cast(ExternKernelSchedulerNode, node)\n                self.codegen_extern_call(node)\n            elif node.is_foreach():\n                node = typing.cast(ForeachKernelSchedulerNode, node)\n                backend_ = self.get_backend(device)\n                from .codegen.cuda_combined_scheduling import CUDACombinedScheduling\n                from .codegen.simd import SIMDScheduling\n\n                if isinstance(backend_, (SIMDScheduling, CUDACombinedScheduling)):\n                    backend = backend_\n                else:\n                    raise AssertionError(f\"{type(self)=}\")\n                backend.codegen_combo_kernel(node)\n            elif isinstance(node, (FusedSchedulerNode, SchedulerNode)):\n                self.get_backend(device).codegen_node(node)\n            else:\n                assert isinstance(node, NopKernelSchedulerNode)\n                node.mark_run()\n\n            if config.triton.debug_sync_kernel:\n                self.get_backend(device).codegen_sync()\n\n            self.available_buffer_names.update(node.get_buffer_names())\n            self.completed_operations.update(node.get_operation_names())\n\n            if not isinstance(node, NopKernelSchedulerNode):\n                device = node.get_device()\n                if (\n                    device is not None\n                    and device.type != \"meta\"\n                    and self.get_backend(device).ready_to_flush()\n                ):\n                    self.flush()\n\n        if self.current_device != self.default_device_context:\n            # when default_device_context is not None, we are codegen\n            # for graph partitions and all nodes must be on\n            # the same default device.\n            assert self.current_device is not None\n            if device_need_guard(self.current_device.type):\n                # exit the outermost CUDA device guard. this is\n                # important for nested indentation codegen-ing.\n                V.graph.wrapper_code.codegen_device_guard_exit()\n\n        self.flush()\n\n    def benchmark_combo_kernel(\n        self, node_list: Sequence[BaseSchedulerNode]\n    ) -> tuple[float, float, list[Optional[str]]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Benchmark fused list of nodes and return the execution time\n        in milliseconds on randomly generated inputs.\n        \"\"\"\n        device = node_list[0].get_device()\n        V.graph.scheduler = self\n        self.current_device = device\n        assert device is not None\n        backend = self.get_backend(device)\n        return backend.benchmark_combo_kernel(node_list)\n\n    def speedup_by_combo_kernel(self, nodes: list[BaseSchedulerNode]) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        If config.benchmark_fusion is False, always return True.\n        Otherwise, return True if fusion can brings speedup.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not config.benchmark_combo_kernel:\n            return True\n\n        subkernel_nodes = nodes\n        device = subkernel_nodes[0].get_device()\n\n        # don't support benchmark fusion for CPU right now.\n        if device is None or device.type == \"cpu\":\n            return True\n\n        from triton.compiler.errors import CompilationError\n\n        ms1, path1_list = 0.0, []\n        for i, snode in enumerate(subkernel_nodes):\n            node_list = snode.get_nodes()\n            # We can not accurately benchmark kernel using atomic_add\n            # due to how we generate random integer inputs.\n            if self._any_atomic_add(node_list):\n                fusion_log.debug(\n                    \"ComboKernel: benchmarking may not accurate due to atomic_add\"\n                )\n\n            try:\n                ms, path = self.benchmark_fused_nodes(node_list)\n                if math.isinf(ms):\n                    fusion_log.debug(\n                        \"ComboKernel benchmark: register spilling of %d-th subkernel\",\n                        i,\n                    )\n                    return False\n            except CompilationError as e:\n                # workaround triton issue: https://github.com/triton-lang/triton/issues/2151\n                if \"Loop-carried variable\" in str(e):\n                    fusion_log.debug(\n                        \"ComboKernel benchmark: return True because of loop-carried variable\"\n                    )\n                    return True  # allow fusion\n                else:\n                    raise\n            ms1 += ms\n            path1_list.append(path)\n\n        try:\n            ms2, ms2_clone, _path2_list = self.benchmark_combo_kernel(subkernel_nodes)\n        except CompilationError as e:\n            # workaround triton issue: https://github.com/triton-lang/triton/issues/2151\n            if \"Loop-carried variable\" in str(e):\n                fusion_log.debug(\n                    \"ComboKernel benchmark: return True because of loop-carried variable\"\n                )\n                return True  # allow fusion\n            else:\n                raise\n\n        # small kernels are very likely to have speedup but hard to benchmark. So we skip benchmarking.\n        small_kernel = ms2 - ms2_clone < 0.3 or ms1 < 0.3\n        if fusion_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\n            if ms1 > ms2 or small_kernel:\n                fusion_log.debug(\n                    \"can fuse (benchmark): fusing causes %sx speedup\",\n                    green_text(f\"{ms1 / ms2:.3f}\"),\n                )\n            else:\n                fusion_log.debug(\n                    \"cannot fuse (benchmark): fusing causes %sx slowdown\",\n                    red_text(f\"{ms1 / ms2:.3f}\"),\n                )\n        # ms1 returned by benchmark_fused_nodes discounted clone time\n        return ms2 - ms2_clone < ms1 or small_kernel\n\n    def get_buffer_layout(self, buf_name: str) -> ir.Layout:\n        buf = self.name_to_buf[buf_name]\n        assert buf.node is not None\n        return buf.node.get_layout()\n\n    def update_zero_dim_cpu_tensor(self) -> None:\n        for node in self.nodes:\n            if node.is_gpu():\n                for read in node.read_writes.reads:\n                    buffer = V.graph.name_to_buffer.get(read.name)\n                    if (\n                        buffer\n                        and get_device_type(buffer) == \"cpu\"\n                        and not isinstance(\n                            buffer.layout, (NoneLayout, MultiOutputLayout)\n                        )\n                        and buffer.get_size() == []\n                    ):\n                        V.graph.zero_dim_cpu_tensor_list.add(read.name)\n\n\nclass BaseScheduling:\n    def __init__(self, scheduler: Optional[Scheduler]):\n        super().__init__()\n        self.scheduler = scheduler\n\n    def free_buffers_in_scheduler(self) -> None:\n        if self.scheduler:\n            self.scheduler.free_buffers()\n\n    def get_backend_features(self, device: torch.device) -> OrderedSet[BackendFeature]:\n        \"\"\"Return a set of .codegen.common.BackendFeature()\"\"\"\n        return OrderedSet()\n\n    def can_fuse_vertical(\n        self, node1: BaseSchedulerNode, node2: BaseSchedulerNode\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check whether node1 and node2 can be vertically fused or not.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def can_fuse_horizontal(\n        self, node1: BaseSchedulerNode, node2: BaseSchedulerNode\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check whether node1 and node2 can be horizontally fused or not.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def can_fuse_multi_outputs_template(\n        self, node1: BaseSchedulerNode, node2: BaseSchedulerNode\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        A Multi-Output Template (referenced in #144012) is a template node\n        with MultiOutputLayout, and its output buffers are instances of MultiOutput.\n        In this context, we verify whether node1 represents the Multi-Output Template\n        and node2 corresponds to one of its outputs. If so, we further check if\n        backend supports this fusion.\n        \"\"\"\n        return False\n\n    def fuse(\n        self, node1: BaseSchedulerNode, node2: BaseSchedulerNode\n    ) -> FusedSchedulerNode:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fuse two nodes\n        \"\"\"\n        if node1.is_foreach() or node2.is_foreach():\n            return ForeachKernelSchedulerNode.fuse(node1, node2)\n        else:\n            return FusedSchedulerNode.fuse(node1, node2)\n\n    def group_fn(\n        self, sizes: Sequence[Sequence[sympy.Expr]]\n    ) -> tuple[tuple[sympy.Expr, ...], ...]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process the iteration sizes in case a transformation needs to be applied.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def codegen_template(\n        self,\n        template_node: BaseSchedulerNode,\n        epilogue_nodes: Sequence[BaseSchedulerNode],\n        prologue_nodes: Sequence[BaseSchedulerNode],\n    ) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Given a template node, generate a kernel.\n\n        This function is only available for triton now. If the third-party backend behaves as a sub-class\n        of TritonScheduling, it can override it or reuse it.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def generate_kernel_code_from_nodes(\n        self,\n        nodes: Sequence[BaseSchedulerNode],\n        benchmark_kernel: bool,\n        hint_override: Optional[int] = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate a kernel given a list of pre-fused nodes.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def codegen_node(self, node: Union[FusedSchedulerNode, SchedulerNode]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate a kernel given a list of pre-fused nodes.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def codegen_sync(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate synchronization code for the kernel. This method depends on the hardware characteristics.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def ready_to_flush(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check whether the backend is requesting the scheduler to flush the generated kernel.\n        If not supported, please return False.\n        \"\"\"\n        return False\n\n    def flush(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Flush the generated kernel and python wrapper code to the source code file.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def benchmark_fused_nodes(\n        self, nodes: Sequence[BaseSchedulerNode]\n    ) -> tuple[float, str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Benchmark fused list of nodes and return the execution time\n        in milliseconds on randomly generated inputs.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def benchmark_codegened_module(self, module: ModuleType) -> tuple[float, str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Benchmark a compiled module and return the execution time\n        in milliseconds on randomly generated inputs.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def get_fusion_pair_priority(\n        self, node1: BaseSchedulerNode, node2: BaseSchedulerNode\n    ) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Return an unsigned integer which represents the priority of this fusion pair.\n        The smaller is with higher priority.\n        \"\"\"\n        return 0\n\n    def benchmark_combo_kernel(\n        self, node_list: Sequence[BaseSchedulerNode]\n    ) -> tuple[float, float, list[Optional[str]]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Benchmark the list of nodes to combine and return the execution time\n        and memory copy time in milliseconds on randomly generated inputs.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "     from collections.abc import Iterator, Sequence",
        "     from types import ModuleType",
        " ",
        "-import weakref",
        "-",
        " import sympy",
        " ",
        " import torch"
      ],
      "newLines": 6,
      "newStart": 23,
      "oldLines": 8,
      "oldStart": 23
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

{
  "file": {
    "content": "                code = \"\".join(code)\n                found = re.search(r\"partitions=\\[(.*)\\]\", code)\n                assert found is not None\n                partitions = found.group(1)\n                num_partitions = len([p for p in partitions.split(\",\") if p])\n                return num_partitions\n\n            @torch.library.custom_op(\"mylib::bar\", mutates_args=())\n            def bar(x: torch.Tensor, flag: int) -> torch.Tensor:\n                return x.clone()\n\n            @bar.register_fake\n            def _(x, flag):\n                return x.clone()\n\n            def f(x, flag):\n                x = x + 1\n                x = bar(x, flag)\n                x = x + 1\n                return x\n\n            x = torch.randn(2, device=\"cuda\")\n            f_compiled = torch.compile(f, mode=\"reduce-overhead\", fullgraph=True)\n            _, code = run_and_get_code(f_compiled, x, True)\n            num_partitions = get_num_partitions(code)\n            self.assertEqual(num_partitions, 1)\n\n            @torch.library.custom_op(\"mylib::baz\", mutates_args=())\n            def baz(x: torch.Tensor, flag: int) -> torch.Tensor:\n                return x.clone()\n\n            @baz.register_fake\n            def _(x, flag):\n                return x.clone()\n\n            def should_partition(x, flag):\n                return flag\n\n            torch._inductor.scheduler.register_should_partition_rule(\n                torch.ops.mylib.baz.default, should_partition\n            )\n\n            def f(x, flag):\n                x = x + 1\n                x = baz(x, flag)\n                x = x + 1\n                return x\n\n            f_compiled = torch.compile(f, mode=\"reduce-overhead\", fullgraph=True)\n            _, code = run_and_get_code(f_compiled, x, True)\n            num_partitions = get_num_partitions(code)\n            self.assertEqual(num_partitions, 2)\n\n            _, code = run_and_get_code(f_compiled, x, False)\n            num_partitions = get_num_partitions(code)\n            self.assertEqual(num_partitions, 1)\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"graph_partition\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_trees\", False)\n        def test_graph_partition_gc(self):\n            def _test_dummy():\n                def foo(x):\n                    return x + 1\n\n                foo = torch.compile(foo)\n                for _ in range(3):\n                    foo(torch.randn(2, 3, device=\"cuda\"))\n\n            _test_dummy()\n            gc.collect()\n            self.assertIsNone(self.get_manager())\n\n        def test_sparsity(self):\n            def foo(view_6, buf31):\n                return aten._sparse_coo_tensor_with_dims_and_tensors(\n                    1,\n                    1,\n                    [1000000, 64],\n                    view_6,\n torch._dynamo.backends.debugging import aot_eager_decomp_partition_with_mode\nfrom torch._dynamo.utils import counters\nfrom torch._functorch._aot_autograd.autograd_cache import AOTAutogradCache\nfrom torch.utils._mode_utils import no_dispatch\nfrom torch.utils._python_dispatch import TorchDispatchMode\n\n\nif IS_WINDOWS and IS_CI:\n    sys.stderr.write(\n        \"Windows CI does not have necessary dependencies for test_torchinductor yet\\n\"\n    )\n    if __name__ == \"__main__\":\n        sys.exit(0)\n    raise unittest.SkipTest(\"requires sympy/functorch/filelock\")\n\nimportlib.import_module(\"functorch\")\nimportlib.import_module(\"filelock\")\n\n\naten = torch.ops.aten\nrequires_multigpu = functools.partial(\n    unittest.skipIf, not TEST_MULTIGPU, \"requires multiple cuda devices\"\n)\nfrom io import StringIO\n\n\ndef get_compile_fn(backend):\n    if backend == \"cudagraphs\":\n        return functools.partial(torch.compile, backend=\"cudagraphs\")\n    else:\n        return functools.partial(torch.compile, mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n\n\nclass capture_stderr(list):\n    \"\"\"\n    Replace sys.stderr with a temporary StringIO\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __enter__(self):\n        self.sys_stderr = sys.stderr\n        self.stringio = StringIO()\n        sys.stderr = self.stringio\n        return self\n\n    def __exit__(self, *args):\n        self.append(str(self.stringio.getvalue()))\n        del self.stringio\n        sys.stderr = self.sys_stderr\n\n\ndef cdata(t):\n    return t.untyped_storage()._cdata\n\n\nclass TestCase(InductorTestCase):\n    @classmethod\n    def setUpClass(cls):\n        super().setUpClass()\n        cls._stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n        cls._stack.enter_context(\n            config.patch(\n                {\n                    \"debug\": True,\n                    \"cpp.min_chunk_size\": 1,\n                    \"triton.autotune_pointwise\": False,  # too slow\n                    \"implicit_fallbacks\": False,\n                }\n            )\n        )\n\n    @classmethod\n    def tearDownClass(cls):\n        cls._stack.close()\n        super().tearDownClass()\n\n    def setUp(self):\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n        super().setUp()\n\n    def tearDown(self):\n        super().tearDown()\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n\nif HAS_CUDA_AND_TRITON:\n\n    def get_all_cudagraph_segments():\n        segments = torch.cuda.memory_snapshot()\n        return [segment for segment in segments if segment[\"segment_pool_id\"] != (0, 0)]\n\n    def all_live_blocks():\n        blocks_addrs = []\n        for segment in get_all_cudagraph_segments():\n            addr = segment[\"address\"]\n            for block in segment[\"blocks\"]:\n                if block[\"state\"] == \"active_allocated\":\n                    blocks_addrs.append(addr)\n                addr += block[\"size\"]\n\n        return blocks_addrs\n\n    def all_live_block_count():\n        return len(all_live_blocks())\n\n    class CudaGraphTreeTests(TestCase):\n        def setUp(self):\n            super().setUp()\n            self.graph_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n            self.graph_stack.enter_context(\n                config.patch(\n                    {\n                        \"triton.cudagraphs\": True,\n                        \"triton.cudagraph_trees\": True,\n                        \"triton.fast_path_cudagraph_asserts\": True,  # too slow\n                        \"triton.slow_path_cudagraph_asserts\": True,\n                    }\n                )\n            )\n            self.graph_stack.enter_context(\n                dynamo_config.patch(automatic_dynamic_shapes=True)\n            )\n            self.device_idx = torch.rand([0], device=\"cuda\").device.index\n            warnings.filterwarnings(\"ignore\")\n\n        def tearDown(self):\n            super().tearDown()\n            torch._dynamo.reset()\n            gc.collect()\n            torch.cuda.empty_cache()\n            self.graph_stack.close()\n\n            self.assertIsNone(self.get_manager())\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 0)\n            self.assertEqual(len(get_all_cudagraph_segments()), 0)\n            warnings.resetwarnings()\n\n        def get_manager(self, device_index=None):\n            return torch._inductor.cudagraph_trees.get_container(\n                self.device_idx if not device_index else device_index\n            ).tree_manager\n\n        def get_roots(self):\n            return self.get_manager().get_roots()\n\n        def curr_node(self):\n            return self.get_manager().current_node\n\n        def get_root_children(self):\n            return [root.num_descendants() for root in self.get_roots()]\n\n        def cudagraphify_impl(\n            self, *args, is_inference=True, is_backward=False, **kwargs\n        ):\n            return tree_cudagraphify_impl(\n                *args,\n                **kwargs,\n                device_index=self.device_idx,\n                is_inference=is_inference,\n                is_backward=is_backward,\n            )\n\n        @staticmethod\n        def run_twc(fn, *args, **kwargs):\n            fn(*args, **kwargs)\n            return fn(*args, **kwargs)\n\n        def num_checkpoints(self):\n            return self.get_manager().debug_checkpointing_counter\n\n        def test_run_simple(self):\n            def foo(x):\n                return x * x * x\n\n            foo_opt = torch.compile(foo)\n            ones = torch.ones([4, 4], device=\"cuda\")\n            zeros = torch.zeros([5, 5], device=\"cuda\")\n            self.run_twc(foo_opt, ones)\n            self.run_twc(foo_opt, zeros)\n            self.assertEqual(self.get_root_children(), [0, 0])\n\n        def check_rng(self):\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo():\n                return torch.rand([20])\n\n            torch.manual_seed(0)\n\n            out = foo()\n            out2 = foo()\n            out3 = foo()\n\n            torch.manual_seed(0)\n\n            self.assertEqual(out, foo())\n            self.assertEqual(out2, foo())\n            self.assertEqual(out3, foo())\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fallback_random\", True)\n        def test_rng_trees(self):\n            self.check_rng()\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_trees\", False)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fallback_random\", True)\n        def test_rng_non_trees(self):\n            self.check_rng()\n\n        def test_mutation_reinplaced(self):\n            import torch.nn as nn\n\n            class Model(nn.Module):\n                def __init__(self) -> None:\n                    super().__init__()\n\n                def forward(self, input, other, out):\n                    input = torch.logical_xor(input=input, other=other, out=out)\n                    return input\n\n            x = torch.rand([1, 2, 1, 4, 9, 7], dtype=torch.float32).cuda()\n            y = torch.rand([1, 2, 1, 4, 9, 7], dtype=torch.float32).cuda()\n            z = torch.rand([1, 2, 1, 4, 9, 7], dtype=torch.float16).cuda()\n\n            model = Model().cuda()\n            eag = model(x, y, z)\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                opt = torch.compile(model.forward, mode=\"reduce-overhead\")(x, y, z)\n\n            FileCheck().check(\n                \"skipping cudagraphs due to mutated inputs (1 instances). Found from\"\n            ).check(\"torch.logical_xor\").run(captured_output[0])\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"], 1)\n\n        @requires_multigpu()\n        @parametrize(\"backend\", (\"inductor\", \"cudagraphs\"))\n        def test_multiple_devices_msg(self, backend):\n            def foo(x, y):\n                return (x + 1, y + 2)\n\n            foo = get_compile_fn(backend)(foo)\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                foo(torch.ones([10], device=\"cuda\"), torch.ones([20]))\n\n            if torch._inductor.config.graph_partition:\n                # graph partition splits on cpu ops\n                self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"], 0)\n            else:\n                FileCheck().check(\n                    \"skipping cudagraphs due to cpu device (arg1_1). Found from\"\n                ).check(\"y + 2\").run(captured_output[0])\n                self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"], 1)\n\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                foo(\n                    torch.ones([10], device=\"cuda:0\"), torch.ones([10], device=\"cuda:1\")\n                )\n\n            FileCheck().check(\"skipping cudagraphs due to multiple devices\").run(\n                captured_output[0]\n            )\n            self.assertEqual(\n                counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"],\n                1 if torch._inductor.config.graph_partition else 2,\n            )\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_skip_dynamic_graphs\", True)\n        def test_skip_symbolic(self):\n            @torch.compile(dynamic=True)\n            def foo(x, y):\n                return x + y\n\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                foo(torch.rand([10], device=\"cuda\"), torch.rand([10], device=\"cuda\"))\n\n            FileCheck().check(\n                \"skipping cudagraphs due to graph with symbolic shapes inputs\"\n            ).check(\"x + y\").run(captured_output[0])\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"], 1)\n\n        @parametrize(\"backend\", (\"inductor\", \"cudagraphs\"))\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_keep_input_mutation\", True)\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_support_input_mutation\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_support_input_mutation\", True)\n        def test_mutation_on_inp(self, backend):\n            def foo(x):\n                x.add_(2)\n                return x\n\n            foo = get_compile_fn(backend)(foo)\n\n            def inp():\n                return torch.ones([10], device=\"cuda\")\n\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                foo(inp())\n\n            FileCheck().check(\n                \"skipping cudagraphs due to mutated inputs (1 instances). Found from\"\n            ).check(\".add_(2)\").run(captured_output[0])\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"], 1)\n\n            # mutation on inp doesn't hit cudagraphs\n            self.assertEqual(len(self.get_manager().roots), 0)\n\n            # mutation on parameters/buffers hits cudagraphs\n            class Mod(torch.nn.Module):\n                def __init__(self) -> None:\n                    super().__init__()\n                    self.buf = torch.ones([10], device=\"cuda\")\n\n                def forward(self, x):\n                    self.buf.add_(x)\n                    return self.buf + x\n\n            def foo(mod, x):\n                return mod(x)\n\n            foo = get_compile_fn(backend)(foo)\n            mod = Mod()\n            mod2 = Mod()\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                self.assertEqual(foo(mod, inp()), mod2(inp()))\n                self.assertEqual(mod.buf, mod2.buf)\n\n            self.assertIsNotNone(self.get_manager())\n\n        @parametrize(\"backend\", (\"inductor\", \"cudagraphs\"))\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_keep_input_mutation\", True)\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_support_input_mutation\", False)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_support_input_mutation\", False)\n        def test_mutation_cudagraph_managed_tensors_config(self, backend):\n            def foo(x):\n                return x + 1\n\n            def mut(x):\n                x.add_(2)\n                return x\n\n            def non_mut(x):\n                return x.add(2)\n\n            mut = get_compile_fn(backend)(mut)\n            foo = get_compile_fn(backend)(foo)\n\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                for i in range(3):\n                    torch.compiler.cudagraph_mark_step_begin()\n                    inp = torch.rand([4], device=\"cuda\")\n\n                    tmp = foo(inp)\n                    mut_out = mut(tmp)\n                    self.assertEqual(mut_out, non_mut(foo(inp)))\n            FileCheck().check_count(\n                \"skipping cudagraphs due to mutated inputs (1 instances). Found from\",\n                1,\n                exactly=True,\n            ).run(captured_output[0])\n\n        @parametrize(\"backend\", (\"inductor\", \"cudagraphs\"))\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_keep_input_mutation\", True)\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_support_input_mutation\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_support_input_mutation\", True)\n        def test_mutation_cudagraph_managed_tensors(self, backend):\n            def foo(x):\n                return x + 1\n\n            def mut(x):\n                x.add_(2)\n                return x\n\n            def non_mut(x):\n                return x.add(2)\n\n            mut = get_compile_fn(backend)(mut)\n            foo = get_compile_fn(backend)(foo)\n\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                for i in range(3):\n                    torch.compiler.cudagraph_mark_step_begin()\n                    inp = torch.rand([4], device=\"cuda\")\n\n                    tmp = foo(inp)\n                    mut_out = mut(tmp)\n                    self.assertEqual(mut_out, non_mut(foo(inp)))\n            FileCheck().check_count(\n                \"skipping cudagraphs due to mutated inputs (1 instances). Found from\",\n                0,\n                exactly=True,\n            ).run(captured_output[0])\n            self.assertTrue(\"cudagraph_skips\" not in counters[\"inductor\"])\n\n            torch.compiler.cudagraph_mark_step_begin()\n            inp = torch.rand([4], device=\"cuda\")\n            tmp = foo(inp)\n            mut_inp = tmp.clone()\n            # in this case, what previously a mutated cudagraph managed tensor is no longer,\n            # now its an input from eager we should fallback to inductor without cudagraphs\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                mut(mut_inp)\n            FileCheck().check(\n                \"skipping cudagraphs due to mutated inputs (1 instances). Found from\"\n            ).check(\"x.add_(2)\").run(captured_output[0])\n            self.assertEqual(mut_inp, non_mut(foo(inp)))\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"], 1)\n\n        @parametrize(\"backend\", (\"inductor\", \"cudagraphs\"))\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_keep_input_mutation\", True)\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_support_input_mutation\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_support_input_mutation\", True)\n        def test_mutation_cudagraph_managed_tensor_warn(self, backend):\n            def foo(x):\n                return x.add_(1)\n\n            def fee(y, z):\n                return z.add(3)\n\n            def inp():\n                return torch.rand([4], device=\"cuda\")\n\n            foo = get_compile_fn(backend)(foo)\n            fee = get_compile_fn(backend)(fee)\n\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                for _ in range(3):\n                    torch.compiler.cudagraph_mark_step_begin()\n                    fee(inp(), foo(inp()))\n            FileCheck().check_count(\n                \"skipping cudagraphs due to mutated inputs (1 instances). Found from\",\n                1,\n                exactly=True,\n            ).run(captured_output[0])\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"], 1)\n\n        @parametrize(\"backend\", (\"inductor\", \"cudagraphs\"))\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_keep_input_mutation\", True)\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"cudagraph_backend_support_input_mutation\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_support_input_mutation\", True)\n        def test_mutation_cudagraph_managed_tensor_warn_only_once(self, backend):\n            def foo(x):\n                return x + 1\n\n            def mut(x):\n                x.add_(2)\n                return x\n\n            def inp():\n                return torch.rand([4], device=\"cuda\")\n\n            mut = get_compile_fn(backend)(mut)\n            foo = get_compile_fn(backend)(foo)\n\n            with capture_stderr() as captured_output:\n                # Should warn for current_node=None\n                mut(inp())\n\n                for i in range(3):\n                    torch.compiler.cudagraph_mark_step_begin()\n                    tmp = foo(inp())\n                    mut(tmp)  # should not warn\n\n                mut_inp = tmp.clone()\n                mut(mut_inp)  # should not warn since mut has warned\n\n            FileCheck().check_count(\n                \"skipping cudagraphs due to mutated inputs (1 instances). Found from\",\n                1,\n                exactly=True,\n            ).run(captured_output[0])\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"], 1)\n\n        def test_index_put(self):\n            def fn(x, y, z):\n                x = torch.zeros_like(x)\n                return x.index_put_([y], z, True)\n\n            fn_c = torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")(fn)\n\n            for i in range(3):\n\n                def args():\n                    x = torch.zeros((512, 512), dtype=torch.bool, device=\"cuda\")\n                    y = torch.arange(512, dtype=torch.int64, device=\"cuda\")\n                    z = torch.ones((512, 512), dtype=torch.bool, device=\"cuda\")\n                    return x, y, z\n\n                if i == 0:\n                    out, code = run_and_get_code(fn_c, *args())\n                    FileCheck().check(\"aten.index_put_\").check_same(\"True\").run(code[0])\n                else:\n                    out = fn_c(*args())\n\n                self.assertEqual(fn(*args()), out)\n\n        def test_function_compiled_multiple_times(self):\n            def foo(x):\n                y = foo2(x)\n                y2 = foo2(y)\n                return y + y2\n\n            def foo2(x):\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                return x * x * x\n\n            foo_opt = torch.compile(foo)\n            ones = torch.ones([4, 4], device=\"cuda\")\n            foo(ones)\n            foo_opt(ones)\n            foo_opt(ones)\n            self.assertEqual(foo_opt(ones), foo(ones))\n            # paths\n            children = self.get_root_children()\n            # one root with two children\n            self.assertEqual(children, [2])\n\n        def test_end_recording_early(self):\n            def foo(x):\n                y = x * x * x\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                z = x + y\n                return z\n\n            @torch.compile\n            def foo2(x):\n                return x + 4\n\n            foo_opt = torch.compile(foo)\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                out = foo_opt(torch.ones([4, 4], device=\"cuda\"))\n                del out\n\n                # when I tried inducing separate recordings via graph break,\n                # the frame kept interfering by keeping outputs alive\n                # this isn't great by simulates the logic.\n                from torch._dynamo.mutation_guard import GenerationTracker\n\n                GenerationTracker.generation -= 1\n\n                out = foo2(torch.ones([4, 4], device=\"cuda\"))\n                del out\n\n            foo_opt(torch.ones([4, 4], device=\"cuda\"))\n\n            # Two separate traces - one has a child, one doesn't\n            self.assertEqual(self.get_root_children(), [1, 0])\n\n        def test_execution_into_recording(self):\n            def foo(x):\n                y = x + x\n\n                if y.sum() > 0:\n                    return y + 10\n                else:\n                    return y - 10\n\n            foo_opt = torch.compile(foo)\n            inp = torch.zeros([4, 4], dtype=torch.float, device=\"cuda\")\n            self.assertEqual(foo_opt(inp), foo(inp))\n            self.assertEqual(foo_opt(inp), foo(inp))\n\n            inp.add_(1)\n            out_eager = foo(inp)\n            out_warmup = foo_opt(inp)\n            self.assertEqual(out_warmup, out_eager)\n            # warmup should be have storage deallocator hooked on\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 1)\n\n            out_live = foo_opt(inp)\n            self.assertEqual(out_live, out_eager)\n\n            # should be in recording mode, with storage deallocator hooked on\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 1)\n            # warmup should have been freed\n            del out_warmup\n            # should be in recording mode, with storage deallocator hooked on\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 1)\n\n            del out_live\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 0)\n\n            out = foo_opt(inp)\n            self.assertEqual(foo(inp), out)\n\n            # should be in execution mode\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 0)\n\n        def test_forward_with_skipped_cudagraphed_backward(self):\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo(x):\n                return x * x * x\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                inp = torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n                out = foo(inp)\n\n                with config.patch(always_complex_memory_overlap_TESTING_ONLY=True):\n                    back_inp = torch.empty_strided([20, 20], [0, 1], device=\"cuda\")\n                    out.backward(back_inp)\n\n            # we should not have cudagraph'd the backwards\n            new_id = self.get_manager().new_graph_id().id\n            self.assertEqual(new_id, 1)\n\n            self.assertFalse(self.get_manager().running_forwards_with_pending_backwards)\n\n        @torch._functorch.config.patch(\"enable_autograd_cache\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fx_graph_cache\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fx_graph_remote_cache\", False)\n        # Currently fx graph cache is turned off for specialize_float=False\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"specialize_float\", True)\n        def test_cache_hit_forward_miss_backward(self):\n            # Test that we don't cache cudagraphs, skipping cudagraphs on backward on a cache miss\n\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo(x):\n                return x * x * x\n\n            # Run forwards, fx graph should cache miss\n            for _ in range(3):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n                counters.clear()\n                FxGraphCache.clear()\n                AOTAutogradCache.clear()\n\n                with config.patch(always_complex_memory_overlap_TESTING_ONLY=True):\n                    inp = torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n                    out = foo(inp)\n                    self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"fxgraph_cache_miss\"], 1)\n\n                    # Reset dynamo and related caches except for FXGraphCache\n                    torch._dynamo.reset()\n                    # Forwards should be a cache hit now, we still skip cudagraphs\n                    inp = torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n                    out = foo(inp)\n                    self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"fxgraph_cache_miss\"], 1)\n                    self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"fxgraph_cache_hit\"], 1)\n\n                    # Run backward without complex memory overlap being set\n\n                # Run the backward without complex memory overlap reason\n                # cache should miss, but cudagraphs should not run\n                # because forward skipped it\n                back_inp = torch.empty_strided([20, 20], [0, 1], device=\"cuda\")\n                out.backward(back_inp)\n                self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"fxgraph_cache_miss\"], 2)\n\n            # Run it one more time, this time AOTAutogradCache will hit\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"aot_autograd\"][\"autograd_cache_miss\"], 2)\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"aot_autograd\"][\"autograd_cache_saved\"], 1)\n\n            torch._dynamo.reset()\n            inp = torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n            out = foo(inp)\n            back_inp = torch.empty_strided([20, 20], [0, 1], device=\"cuda\")\n            out.backward(back_inp)\n\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"aot_autograd\"][\"autograd_cache_hit\"], 1)\n\n            # we should not have cudagraph'd anything\n            assert self.get_manager() is None\n\n        @torch._functorch.config.patch(\"enable_autograd_cache\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fx_graph_cache\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fx_graph_remote_cache\", False)\n        # Currently fx graph cache is turned off for specialize_float=False\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"specialize_float\", True)\n        @requires_multigpu()\n        def test_cached_boxed_forward_device_index(self):\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo(x):\n                return x * x * x\n\n            # Run with device index 1 so that we can see\n            # on a cache hit we stay on device index 1\n            with torch.cuda._DeviceGuard(1):\n                torch.cuda.set_device(1)\n\n                inp = torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n                out = foo(inp)\n                self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"fxgraph_cache_miss\"], 1)\n                # Compile the backward and save to cache\n                back_inp = torch.empty_strided([20, 20], [0, 1], device=\"cuda\")\n                out.backward(back_inp)\n                self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"fxgraph_cache_miss\"], 2)\n                self.assertEqual(counters[\"aot_autograd\"][\"autograd_cache_miss\"], 1)\n                self.assertEqual(counters[\"aot_autograd\"][\"autograd_cache_saved\"], 1)\n\n                # Reset dynamo and rerun a few times\n                for i in range(3):\n                    torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n                    inp = torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n                    out = foo(inp)\n                    # Should cache hit each time; boxed_forward_device_index should still be set properly to 1\n                    self.assertEqual(\n                        counters[\"aot_autograd\"][\"autograd_cache_hit\"], i + 1\n                    )\n                    back_inp = torch.empty_strided([20, 20], [0, 1], device=\"cuda\")\n                    out.backward(back_inp)\n\n            # After everything, we should have cudagraphs on device 1\n            self.assertTrue(self.get_manager(device_index=0) is None)\n            self.assertFalse(self.get_manager(device_index=1) is None)\n\n        @torch._functorch.config.patch(\"enable_autograd_cache\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fx_graph_cache\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fx_graph_remote_cache\", False)\n        # Currently fx graph cache is turned off for specialize_float=False\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"specialize_float\", True)\n        def test_backward_gets_cached_cudagraphs(self):\n            # We pass cpu tensors to foo and save that into the cache\n            # On a subsequent run in a new process, cudagraphs should be\n            # disabled properly on both forward and backwards runs.\n\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo(x):\n                return x * x * x\n\n            torch._dynamo.reset()\n            counters.clear()\n            FxGraphCache.clear()\n            AOTAutogradCache.clear()\n\n            # Use cpu device to disable cudagraphs during compilation\n            inp = torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cpu\", requires_grad=True)\n            out = foo(inp)\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"fxgraph_cache_miss\"], 1)\n\n            back_inp = torch.empty_strided([20, 20], [0, 1], device=\"cpu\")\n            out.backward(back_inp)\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"fxgraph_cache_miss\"], 2)\n\n            # Run again on new process\n            torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n            # Forward and backward should also disable cudagraphs without compilation\n            inp = torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cpu\", requires_grad=True)\n            out = foo(inp)\n            # AOTAutogradCache will load the forward and the backward from cache immediately, so fx_graph_cache_hit will equal 2\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"inductor\"][\"fxgraph_cache_hit\"], 2)\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"aot_autograd\"][\"autograd_cache_hit\"], 1)\n            torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n            back_inp = torch.empty_strided([20, 20], [0, 1], device=\"cpu\")\n            out.backward(back_inp)\n\n            # we should not have cudagraph'd anything\n            assert self.get_manager() is None\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.skip_cudagraph_warmup\", True)\n        @torch._functorch.config.patch(\"enable_autograd_cache\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fx_graph_cache\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"fx_graph_remote_cache\", False)\n        # Currently fx graph cache is turned off for specialize_float=False\n        @torch._dynamo.config.patch(\"specialize_float\", True)\n        def test_cached_forward_backward(self):\n            counters.clear()\n            AOTAutogradCache.clear()\n            FxGraphCache.clear()\n\n            @torch.compile\n            def foo(x):\n                torch.manual_seed(0)\n                y = x * 2\n                return torch.sin(y) * torch.nn.functional.dropout(x, p=0.4)\n\n            inp = torch.rand([4, 4], requires_grad=True, device=\"cuda\")\n            inp2 = inp.detach().clone().requires_grad_(True)\n            out = foo(inp)\n\n            out.sum().backward()\n\n            self.assertEqual(self.get_root_children(), [1])\n\n            # the three saved tensors should die in the backward\n            # we kept alive the output\n            self.assertEqual(self.curr_node().expected_dead_indices_before_graph, [])\n            if torch._inductor.config.graph_partition:\n                self.assertEqual(\n                    self.curr_node().expected_dead_indices_after_graph,\n                    [(0, 0), (0, 2)],\n                )\n            else:\n                self.assertEqual(\n                    self.curr_node().expected_dead_indices_after_graph,\n                    [(0, 1), (0, 2)],\n                )\n            self.assertFalse(self.get_manager().new_graph_id().id == 0)\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"aot_autograd\"][\"autograd_cache_miss\"], 1)\n\n            # Reset dynamo and rerun. We should see a cache hit now\n            torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n            out2 = foo(inp2)\n            out2.sum().backward()\n            self.assertEqual(out, out2)\n            self.assertEqual(inp.grad, inp2.grad)\n\n            self.assertEqual(self.get_root_children(), [1])\n            self.assertFalse(self.get_manager().new_graph_id().id == 0)\n            self.assertEqual(counters[\"aot_autograd\"][\"autograd_cache_hit\"], 1)\n\n        @parametrize(\"backend\", (\"inductor\", \"cudagraphs\"))\n        def test_forward_backward_not_called(self, backend):\n            def foo(x, y):\n                x_out = x * x * x\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                y_out = y * y * y\n                return x_out, y_out\n\n            foo = get_compile_fn(backend)(foo)\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                inps = [\n                    torch.rand([20, 20], requires_grad=True, device=\"cuda\")\n                    for _ in range(2)\n                ]\n                x_out, y_out = foo(inps[0], inps[1])\n                x_out.sum().backward()\n\n            self.assertFalse(self.get_manager().running_forwards_with_pending_backwards)\n\n            # we should not have cudagraph'd the y backward\n            new_id = self.get_manager().new_graph_id().id\n            self.assertEqual(new_id, 3)\n\n        def _test_unaligned_static_input_impl(self, expected_clones):\n            def fn(x, y):\n                return (x + y,)\n\n            def get_aligned_inputs():\n                return [torch.rand([5, 5], device=\"cuda\") for _ in range(2)]\n\n            mod = make_fx(fn)(*get_aligned_inputs())\n\n            mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode()\n\n            with mode:\n                inps = [torch.rand([6, 5], device=\"cuda\")[1:] for _ in range(2)]\n\n            compiled_f = compile_fx_inner(\n                mod, inps, static_input_idxs=[0], cudagraphs=True\n            )\n\n            def get_unaligned_inputs():\n                return [torch.rand([6, 5], device=\"cuda\")[1:] for _ in range(2)]\n\n            class CloneCounterMode(TorchDispatchMode):\n                def __init__(self) -> None:\n                    self.count = 0\n\n                def __torch_dispatch__(self, func, types, args=(), kwargs=None):\n                    kwargs = {} if kwargs is None else kwargs\n                    self.count += func is torch.ops.aten.clone.default\n                    return func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                with CloneCounterMode() as m:\n                    compiled_f(get_unaligned_inputs())\n                    self.assertEqual(m.count, expected_clones)\n\n                    compiled_f(get_aligned_inputs())\n                    self.assertEqual(m.count, expected_clones)\n\n        def test_unaligned_static_input_trees(self):\n            self._test_unaligned_static_input_impl(expected_clones=0)\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_trees\", False)\n        def test_unaligned_static_input_non_trees(self):\n            self._test_unaligned_static_input_impl(expected_clones=0)\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraphs\", False)\n        def test_unaligned_static_input_no_cudagraphs(self):\n            self._test_unaligned_static_input_impl(expected_clones=0)\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"graph_partition\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"implicit_fallbacks\", True)\n        def test_graph_partition_custom_rule(self):\n            def get_num_partitions(code):\n                code = \"\".join(code)\n                found = re.search(r\"partitions=\\[(.*)\\]\", code)\n                assert found is not None\n                partitions = found.group(1)\n                num_partitions = len([p for p in partitions.split(\",\") if p])\n                return num_partitions\n\n            @torch.library.custom_op(\"mylib::bar\", mutates_args=())\n            def bar(x: torch.Tensor, flag: int) -> torch.Tensor:\n                return x.clone()\n\n            @bar.register_fake\n            def _(x, flag):\n                return x.clone()\n\n            def f(x, flag):\n                x = x + 1\n                x = bar(x, flag)\n                x = x + 1\n                return x\n\n            x = torch.randn(2, device=\"cuda\")\n            f_compiled = torch.compile(f, mode=\"reduce-overhead\", fullgraph=True)\n            _, code = run_and_get_code(f_compiled, x, True)\n            num_partitions = get_num_partitions(code)\n            self.assertEqual(num_partitions, 1)\n\n            @torch.library.custom_op(\"mylib::baz\", mutates_args=())\n            def baz(x: torch.Tensor, flag: int) -> torch.Tensor:\n                return x.clone()\n\n            @baz.register_fake\n            def _(x, flag):\n                return x.clone()\n\n            def should_partition(x, flag):\n                return flag\n\n            torch._inductor.scheduler.register_should_partition_rule(\n                torch.ops.mylib.baz.default, should_partition\n            )\n\n            def f(x, flag):\n                x = x + 1\n                x = baz(x, flag)\n                x = x + 1\n                return x\n\n            f_compiled = torch.compile(f, mode=\"reduce-overhead\", fullgraph=True)\n            _, code = run_and_get_code(f_compiled, x, True)\n            num_partitions = get_num_partitions(code)\n            self.assertEqual(num_partitions, 2)\n\n            _, code = run_and_get_code(f_compiled, x, False)\n            num_partitions = get_num_partitions(code)\n            self.assertEqual(num_partitions, 1)\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"graph_partition\", True)\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.cudagraph_trees\", False)\n        def test_graph_partition_gc(self):\n            def _test_dummy():\n                def foo(x):\n                    return x + 1\n\n                foo = torch.compile(foo)\n                for _ in range(3):\n                    foo(torch.randn(2, 3, device=\"cuda\"))\n\n            _test_dummy()\n            gc.collect()\n            self.assertIsNone(self.get_manager())\n\n        def test_sparsity(self):\n            def foo(view_6, buf31):\n                return aten._sparse_coo_tensor_with_dims_and_tensors(\n                    1,\n                    1,\n                    [1000000, 64],\n                    view_6,\n                    buf31,\n                    dtype=torch.float32,\n                    layout=torch.sparse_coo,\n                    device=\"cuda\",\n                    pin_memory=None,\n                )\n\n            foo_opt = torch.compile(foo)\n\n            view_6 = torch.zeros([1, 102397], dtype=torch.int64, device=\"cuda\")\n            buf31 = torch.rand([102397, 64], device=\"cuda\")\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                self.assertEqual(foo_opt(view_6, buf31), foo(view_6, buf31))\n\n        def test_accumulate_multiple_recordings(self):\n            def foo(x):\n                y = x + x + x\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                if y.sum() <= 0:\n                    return y\n                else:\n                    return y * 10\n\n            foo_opt = torch.compile(foo)\n\n            # two separate compilations & recordings\n            out1 = self.run_twc(foo_opt, torch.zeros([5], device=\"cuda\"))\n\n            # out1 gets manually freed\n            out2 = self.run_twc(foo_opt, torch.zeros([6], device=\"cuda\"))\n\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 1)\n\n            out3 = self.run_twc(foo_opt, torch.ones([5], device=\"cuda\"))\n\n            self.assertEqual(out3, foo(torch.ones([5], device=\"cuda\")))\n\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 1)\n            del out1, out2\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 1)\n\n            del out3\n            gc.collect()\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 0)\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"freezing\", True)\n        def test_constant_output(self):\n            class Mod(torch.nn.Module):\n                def __init__(self) -> None:\n                    super().__init__()\n                    self.param = torch.nn.Parameter(\n                        torch.tensor([float(i) for i in range(10)], device=\"cuda\")\n                    )\n\n                def forward(self, inp):\n                    return self.param, self.param[0:2], inp + 2\n\n            inp = torch.tensor([2], device=\"cuda\")\n            m = Mod()\n            with torch.no_grad():\n                out_eager = m(inp)\n\n                m_comp = torch.compile(m)\n                for _ in range(3):\n                    self.assertEqual(out_eager, m_comp(inp))\n\n        def test_live_outputs_multiple_graphs(self):\n            def foo(x):\n                x = x + x + x\n                y = x + 1\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                z = x * x\n                if z.sum() > 0:\n                    return y + 1\n                else:\n                    return y\n\n            foo_opt = torch.compile(foo)\n\n            self.run_twc(foo_opt, torch.zeros([5], device=\"cuda\"))\n            self.assertEqual(self.num_checkpoints(), 0)\n            out = self.run_twc(foo_opt, torch.ones([5], device=\"cuda\"))\n\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 1)\n\n            del out\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 0)\n\n            # we need to checkpoint from function to warmup y + 1,\n            # and then again to record it\n            self.assertEqual(self.num_checkpoints(), 2)\n\n        def test_expanded_inputs(self):\n            x = torch.rand(1, 512, device=\"cuda\").expand(4, 512)\n\n            def foo(x):\n                return x + 4 + torch.ones([4, 512], device=\"cuda\")\n\n            foo_opt = torch.compile()(foo)\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                self.assertEqual(foo_opt(x), foo(x))\n\n            self.assertFalse(self.get_manager().new_graph_id().id == 0)\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"triton.skip_cudagraph_warmup\", True)\n        def test_tensor_dies_between_checkpoint(self):\n            def foo(args):\n                x = args[0]\n                args.clear()\n                return x + 1, x + 2\n\n            inp = torch.rand([4], device=\"cuda\")\n            inp_list = [inp]\n            foo_cg = self.cudagraphify_impl(foo, inp_list, ())\n            foo_cg(inp_list)\n            foo_cg([inp])\n\n            out1, out2 = foo_cg([inp])\n            inp = [out1]\n\n            del out1, out2\n\n            def foo2(args):\n                x = args[0]\n                args.clear()\n                return [x * x * x]\n\n            self.assertEqual(self.num_checkpoints(), 0)\n            foo2_cg = self.cudagraphify_impl(foo2, inp, ())\n\n            x = foo2_cg(inp)[0]\n\n            self.assertEqual(self.num_checkpoints(), 1)\n            # out2 dies between the previous recording and the new one,\n            # need to be manually deallocated after the checkpoint\n\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 1)\n            del x\n            self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 0)\n\n        def test_aliased_storage_single_weakref(self):\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo(x):\n                x = x * 20\n                x_alias = x[0]\n                y = x * 10\n                y_alias = y[0]\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                ind = torch.tensor(4, device=\"cuda\")\n                x_alias2 = x[ind:]\n                y_alias2 = y[ind:]\n                return x, x_alias, x_alias2, y_alias, y_alias2\n\n            for _ in range(4):\n                outs = foo(torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cuda\"))\n\n                ptr_to_ref = {\n                    out.untyped_storage().data_ptr(): out.untyped_storage()._cdata\n                    for out in outs\n                }\n\n                self.assertEqual(len(ptr_to_ref), 2)\n                for out in outs:\n                    self.assertEqual(\n                        ptr_to_ref[out.untyped_storage().data_ptr()],\n                        out.untyped_storage()._cdata,\n                    )\n                del outs\n                del out\n\n            node = self.get_manager().current_node\n            self.assertEqual(len(list(node.path_live_weakrefs())), 0)\n            self.assertFalse(self.get_manager().new_graph_id().id == 0)\n\n        def test_aliasing_static_ref(self):\n            class Mod(torch.nn.Linear):\n                def forward(self, x):\n                    return self.weight.T @ x, self.weight.T, self.weight[0:4]\n\n            m = Mod(10, 10).cuda()\n\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo(mod, x):\n                return mod(x)\n\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo2(x):\n                return x[2:]\n\n            param_c = cdata(m.weight)\n            for _ in range(3):\n                x = torch.rand([10, 10], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n                torch.compiler.cudagraph_mark_step_begin()\n                out1, alias_1, alias_2 = foo(m, x)\n                self.assertEqual(len({param_c, cdata(alias_1), cdata(alias_2)}), 1)\n\n                out2 = foo2(out1)\n                out2.sum().backward()\n                self.assertEqual(cdata(out1), cdata(out2))\n                m.weight.grad = None\n                m.bias.grad = None\n\n            node = self.curr_node()\n            first_node = next(node._path_from_root)\n            if torch._inductor.config.graph_partition:\n                # graph partition may changed the order of outputs\n                self.assertFalse(first_node.unaliased_in_all_paths[1])\n                self.assertTrue(first_node.cached_tensor_outputs[1] is None)\n            else:\n                self.assertFalse(first_node.unaliased_in_all_paths[0])\n                self.assertTrue(first_node.cached_tensor_outputs[0] is None)\n\n        @torch._inductor.config.patch(\"implicit_fallbacks\", True)\n        def test_multinomial(self):\n            def sample_multinomial(probs, num_samples, replacement=True):\n                return torch.multinomial(probs, num_samples, replacement=replacement)\n\n            # Create and prepare probability tensor on GPU\n            probs = torch.tensor([0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4]).cuda()\n            probs = probs / probs.sum()\n\n            # Sample using the function\n            num_skipped = counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"]\n\n            with torch._dynamo.utils.preserve_rng_state():\n                samples = self.run_twc(\n                    sample_multinomial, probs, num_samples=5, replacement=True\n                )\n\n            with torch._dynamo.utils.preserve_rng_state():\n                samples_compiled = self.run_twc(\n                    torch.compile(sample_multinomial),\n                    probs,\n                    num_samples=5,\n                    replacement=True,\n                )\n\n            self.assertEqual(samples, samples_compiled)\n            self.assertEqual(num_skipped, counters[\"inductor\"][\"cudagraph_skips\"])\n\n        @skipIfRocm\n        def test_checkpointing_resets_persistent_refs(self):\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo(x):\n                return x @ x\n\n            def inp():\n                return torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=False)\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                foo(inp())\n\n            self.assertEqual(self.num_checkpoints(), 0)\n\n            out = foo(inp())\n            out_id = id(out)\n            del out\n            self.assertEqual(id(foo(inp())), out_id)\n\n            @torch.compile(mode=\"reduce-overhead\")\n            def foo2(x):\n                return x[0], x @ x\n\n            for i in range(2):\n                out = foo(inp())\n\n                from torch._dynamo.mutation_guard import GenerationTracker\n\n                GenerationTracker.generation -= 1\n\n                out_alias, out2 = foo2(out)\n                del out_alias\n\n                self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 2)\n                del out\n                self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 1)\n                del out2\n                self.assertEqual(all_live_block_count(), 0)\n\n                self.assertEqual(self.num_checkpoints(), i + 1)\n\n            new_out = foo(inp())\n            curr_node = self.curr_node()\n            self.assertFalse(curr_node.unaliased_in_all_paths[0])\n            self.assertFalse(out_id == id(new_out))\n\n        def test_aliased_static_parameter(self):\n            inp = torch.rand([20, 20], device=\"cuda\")\n\n            def foo(args):\n                x = args[0]\n                args.clear()\n                return (x[0],)\n\n            foo_cg = self.cudagraphify_impl(foo, [inp], (0,))\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                out = foo_cg([inp])[0]\n                self.assertEqual(cdata(inp), cdata(out))\n\n            node = self.curr_node()\n            self.assertEqual(node.cached_tensor_outputs, [None])\n            self.assertEqual(node.unaliased_in_all_paths, [False])\n\n        def test_warmup_stream_sync(self):\n            def foo(args):\n                x = args[0]\n                args.clear()\n                x_orig = x\n                for _ in range(100):\n                    x = x @ x\n                return (x,)\n\n            inp = torch.rand([4096, 4096], device=\"cuda\")\n            ref = foo([inp])[0]\n            torch.cuda.synchronize()\n\n            user_stream = torch.cuda.Stream()\n            with torch.cuda.stream(user_stream):\n                foo_cg = self.cudagraphify_impl(foo, [inp], (0,))\n                out = foo_cg([inp])[0]\n                y = out + 1\n                self.assertEqual(y, ref + 1)\n\n        def test_unaligned_static_parameter(self):\n            def gen_inp():\n                inp = torch.ones([20], device=\"cuda\")\n                return [inp[1:]]\n\n            def foo(args):\n                x = args[0]\n                args.clear()\n                return (x + x,)\n\n            foo_cg = self.cudagraphify_impl(foo, gen_inp(), (0,))\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                out = foo_cg(gen_inp())\n                self.assertEqual(out, foo(gen_inp()))\n                del out\n\n            node = self.curr_node()\n            self.assertEqual(node.static_input_data_ptrs, [None])\n\n        def test_amp_cache_disabled(self):\n            @torch.compile()\n            def foo(x):\n                return x + x\n\n            for _ in range(3):\n                out = foo(torch.rand([4, 4], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True))\n\n            # amp cache for cudagraph outputs should be disabled\n            t2 = torch.rand([4, 4], device=\"cuda\")\n\n            with torch.cuda.amp.autocast():\n                run_once = out @ t2\n\n                out.detach().zero_()\n\n                run_twice = out @ t2\n\n                self.assertNotEqual(run_once, run_twice)\n\n        def test_remove_hooks_on_cached_tensors(self):\n            @torch.compile()\n            def foo(x):\n                return x * x\n\n            inp = torch.rand([4], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n\n            for _ in range(5):\n                out = foo(inp)\n                self.assertIsNone(out._backward_hooks)\n                out.register_hook(lambda: None)\n\n            # today, torch.compile never outputs a leaf tensor which is the only\n            # tensor that can register _post_accumulate_grad_hooks\n            # add this as a preventative test\n\n            @torch.compile()\n            def foo(x):\n                return torch.rand([4], device=\"cuda\", requires_grad=True)\n\n            for _ in range(5):\n                out = foo(inp)\n                self.assertIsNone(out._post_accumulate_grad_hooks)\n
